verses.commentary: Thirteen years of silence after Ishmael's birth. Abram is now 99. God Almighty (El Shaddai) emphasizes power, possibly meaning God of the mountain or God of sufficiency. Walk before me (not just with me as in 5:22) means live in my sight. Faithfully and blameless means wholeheartedly, with integrity.
God renews the covenant. Between me and you is emphatic. Greatly increase suggests multiplication beyond imagination.
Abram's posture. Facedown indicates awe, worship, submission. The proper response to divine revelation.
As for me sets God's commitment. Father of many nations (not just one). Abram's fatherhood extends beyond Israel to multiple peoples (through Ishmael, Esau, and others).
Name change. Abram (exalted father) becomes Abraham (father of many). The addition of the heh from YHWH marks divine ownership. Names signify identity and destiny. The name change is prophetic.
Very fruitful echoes the creation mandate (1:28). Nations (plural) and kings emphasize royal lineage. This anticipates Davidic monarchy and ultimately the Messiah.
Everlasting covenant. Permanence. Multi-generational. The essence: to be your God. This is the covenant formula repeated throughout Scripture. Relationship is the goal.
Land promise reaffirmed. Everlasting possession. Currently a foreigner, but will possess it. And I will be their God repeats the covenant essence.
As for you balances As for me (verse 4). God's part and human part. Keep my covenant means guard, observe. Covenant requires response.
The covenant sign. Circumcision is the physical mark. Every male, no exceptions. This is the identifying sign of covenant membership.

Sign like the rainbow (9:12) or Sabbath (Exodus 31:13). Circumcision marks the body. It is permanent, painful, and personal. It affects the organ of procreation, connecting to the promise of descendants.
Eight days old. Specific timing. Includes servants and foreigners. Covenant community extends beyond bloodline. Entry into covenant is by sign, not just birth.
Must be circumcised, imperative. In your flesh makes it physical and permanent. Everlasting emphasizes perpetuity.
Consequence of refusal. Cut off can mean death or exclusion. Has broken means violated. Covenant membership is serious.

Sarai also receives a name change. Sarai (my princess) becomes Sarah (princess). Both mean princess, but Sarah is universalized, not possessive. She is not just Abram's princess but princess in her own right.
Sarah will have a son. Explicit promise. She will be the mother of nations. Kings will come from her. Sarah is elevated to co-participant in the promise. The son must come from her, not just Abraham.

Abraham's reaction. He falls facedown in worship but also laughs. The root is tsachaq, which becomes Isaac's name. Laughter of joy or incredulity? Both. The question reveals the absurdity. Hundred and ninety years old. Biologically impossible.
Abraham's plea. He has bonded with Ishmael for 13 years. If only expresses a wish. Ishmael is a real son. Abraham asks God to accept Ishmael as the heir. Understandable but not God's plan.

God corrects. Yes, but. Sarah will bear. Isaac means he laughs. The name commemorates Abraham's laughter. The covenant will be with Isaac, not Ishmael. Everlasting covenant for his descendants specifies the line of promise.

Grace for Ishmael. I have heard you shows God's response to Abraham's plea. Ishmael will be blessed, fruitful, multiplied. Twelve rulers are promised (fulfilled in 25:12-16). Great nation, but not the covenant nation. God honors Abraham's love for Ishmael.
But clarifies. The covenant is specifically with Isaac. By this time next year gives a concrete timeline. One year from now. Faith has a countdown.
The encounter ends. God went up suggests visible theophany. God ascends. The revelation is complete.
Immediate obedience. That very day emphasizes urgency. Abraham circumcises Ishmael, himself, and every male. As God told him shows exact compliance.
Abraham's age repeated. Circumcision at 99 is costly. Pain and vulnerability. Obedience regardless of personal cost.
Ishmael's age. Thirteen, on the cusp of adulthood. This becomes the Jewish bar mitzvah age.
Both father and son together. Shared experience. That very day repeated for emphasis.
Total compliance. No exceptions. The entire household enters the covenant. Communal obedience.
